task_completion.description: Open the CAMEO Chemicals website in a web browser
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window is open and navigated to https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/.

task_completion.description: View the 'Protective Clothing' section for the specified chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent viewing the 'Protective Clothing' section on the CAMEO Chemicals page for at least one of the target chemicals (Acetone, Chlorine, Hydrogen Cyanide, Toluene, or Phosgene).

task_completion.description: Correctly classify Acetone and Toluene in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report shows Acetone and Toluene classified as LEVEL_B, and indicates that SCBA (Positive Pressure SCBA) is recommended/required for both.

task_completion.description: Correctly classify Chlorine, Hydrogen Cyanide, and Phosgene in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report shows Chlorine, Hydrogen Cyanide, and Phosgene classified as LEVEL_A, and indicates that SCBA (Positive Pressure SCBA) is recommended/required for all three.

task_completion.description: Save the report to the correct path with the specified format
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file being saved to /home/ga/Documents/protective_clothing_report.txt, and the content includes the required format starting with 'CHEMICAL: [' or similar.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to access the database
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with a real web browser (e.g., Firefox, Chromium) to search the CAMEO database, rather than using a script to fetch data in the background.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script with hardcoded answers
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent manually gathers information from the website and writes it to the report, rather than executing a python or bash script containing the pre-written correct classifications.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Acetone (UN 1090) and Toluene (UN 1294) must be classified as LEVEL_B. Chlorine (UN 1017), Hydrogen Cyanide (UN 1051), and Phosgene (UN 1076) must be classified as LEVEL_A. Positive Pressure SCBA is required for ALL FIVE chemicals. The final report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/protective_clothing_report.txt.
